paragraphs.text: Hello! I'm Sufiana, an Information Systems graduate with a strong focus on System Analysis and structured project execution. I'm particularly interested in how data, clear requirements, and well-defined processes contribute to building effective digital solutions.

highlights.text: data, clear requirements, and well-defined processes

paragraphs.text: As a Certified System Analyst (BNSP), I have been involved in managing digital initiatives in dynamic and professional IT environments, working closely with cross-functional teams under Agile practices. My experience spans product planning, requirement analysis, documentation, and ensuring that development objectives remain clear and measurable.

highlights.text: digital initiatives in dynamic and professional IT environments

paragraphs.text: During my internship as an Associate Product Manager and while leading a website development project, I developed a practical understanding of stakeholder coordination, scope control, and balancing time, quality, and resources — while maintaining structured documentation throughout the project lifecycle.

I enjoy translating complex needs into organized system flows and actionable plans. I also build web-based tools and explore automation and AI-powered solutions to streamline repetitive processes and improve operational efficiency.

highlights.text: translating complex needs into organized system flows

toeflPlatform.title: TOEFL ITP Practice Website
toeflPlatform.description: Integrated course management website application for TOEFL ITP enrollment, payment, and self-learning modules.
toeflPlatform.fullDescription:
The registration process for the TOEFL ITP course at LaC, from enrollment to payment before the start of learning, is currently not integrated. Registration is done via Google Form, proof of payment upload is done in a separate form, confirmation is done manually via WhatsApp, and data management is handled through Excel. Additionally, there is a need to integrate with a self-learning module within a single application. As a result, the process is prone to human error (incorrect input, duplicate data) and is inefficient—participants must wait for manual confirmation, while the administrative burden increases drastically as the number of registrants continues to rise. This project aims to build an integrated course management module that connects the enrollment-to-payment process with self-learning in one application.

I developed the back-end of this course management module using Laravel, with API testing conducted through Postman. The development followed an iterative-incremental approach, during which I designed various UML diagrams to guide the process, including use case diagrams, activity diagrams, sequence diagrams, and class diagrams, complemented by use case scenarios to detail the flow of each use case. These artifacts helped ensure that the system architecture aligned with the functional requirements and supported a structured, phased delivery.
toeflPlatform.tools: Laravel, MySQL, Postman, Visual Paradigm, Google Docs, Ms.Word

sa.caption: Use Case Scenario - Initial Score Selection
Use Case Diagram - Course Management Module

techWriting.caption: API Collection - Initial Score Submission History API Response

netflixDtDashboard.title: NETFLIX Shows Data Breakdown Dashboard
netflixDtDashboard.description: Data visualization dashboard exploring Netflix content trends, genres, and release patterns.
netflixDtDashboard.fullDescription:
This project explores Netflix's content library through data visualization, uncovering trends in content types, release years, genres, and country distributions. The goal was to gain insights into how Netflix's content strategy has evolved over time.

Using a publicly available dataset from Kaggle, I performed data cleaning and transformation using Power Query within Power BI to prepare the data for analysis. I then built an interactive dashboard using Power BI to visualize key metrics—such as content distribution by type, release trends over time, top categories, average duration, and country-level insights.

The dashboard enables users to explore patterns in Netflix's catalog, providing a clear view of how content has diversified across regions, formats, and categories over time. This project demonstrates my ability to work with open datasets, perform exploratory data analysis, and translate raw data into meaningful visual insights.

data.caption: Netflix Dashboard - Content Distribution, Trends & Category Insights

helpdeskMonitoring.title: Helpdesk Monitoring Dashboard
helpdeskMonitoring.description: Analytical dashboard for monitoring WhatsApp-based helpdesk performance using Tableau.
helpdeskMonitoring.fullDescription:
The LAA FRI WhatsApp helpdesk is currently managed manually, making it difficult for Admins to monitor the number of inquiries, response status, and trends. Manual data is often inaccurate and fragmented, hindering decision-making for service improvements. Key issues include limited real-time monitoring, lack of trend analysis, and inefficient decision-making due to unintegrated data. As a result, many inquiries go unnoticed, and recurring issues persist.

This project builds an automated monitoring system using Tableau, with data designed based on SLA requirements and structured within a SQL database. I identified business needs, designed the star schema, and developed an interactive dashboard that provides real-time visibility to Admins.

Currently, the dashboard uses synthetic data generated with Python based on operational helpdesk scenarios, as direct access to real-time data is not yet available. However, the SQL database structure and star schema are designed to be ready for future integration with helpdesk applications—such as a chatbot or ticketing system—enabling real-time data once the source becomes available. The dashboard design was validated through a demo session with LAA representatives, who confirmed that the visualizations and metrics align with their operational needs. While the current dashboard uses synthetic data, the design has been approved as meeting their requirements.
helpdeskMonitoring.tools: Tableau, SQL, Python, Draw.io, Ms.Word, Google Docs

financialTracker.title: Personal Financial Tracker
financialTracker.description: Excel-based personal finance tracker for income, expense tracking, budgeting, and forecasting.
financialTracker.fullDescription:
This personal finance tracker was developed to help manage daily income and expenses, with a structured approach to budgeting and financial planning. The tracker is designed to provide clear visibility into cash flow, enabling users to monitor spending patterns, track savings, and make informed financial decisions.

I designed the tracker structure based on personal financial needs, with configurable categories for income and expense streams—distinguishing sources such as bank accounts or other income channels. The budgeting framework allows users to set custom percentage allocations, managed within a dedicated category management section. This analytical approach reflects a business analysis perspective in understanding user needs and defining functional requirements.

The dashboard suite consists of two main views. The Financial Overview Dashboard provides a high-level snapshot of daily and monthly financial activity, highlighting income vs expense trends and budget allocation. The Financial Analysis & Forecasting Dashboard offers deeper insights, including target vs actual budget tracking, expense category breakdowns, and predictive forecasting based on historical patterns. Structured within Excel using tables, formulas, and pivot tables, this tracker delivers a practical solution for personal financial management—combining structured data design with intuitive visualizations to support better decision-making.

data.caption: Financial Overview Dashboard - Daily & Monthly Financial Snapshot
Financial Analysis & Forecasting Dashboard - Detailed Analysis & Prediction

travelWebPM.title: Travel Website Project
travelWebPM.description: Full-stack travel booking website with integrated project management for operational efficiency.
travelWebPM.fullDescription:
PT Windira Cipta Mandiri is a company specializing in government and official travel services. With increasing growth in tour reservations, the company needed to improve operational efficiency—particularly in booking, scheduling, and payment processing. Currently, customers still make reservations manually by contacting the company directly, which is inefficient, time-consuming, and prone to human error.

This project builds a web-based booking platform to address these challenges, enabling customers to make reservations flexibly without waiting for phone queues or visiting the office. I developed the full-stack application using Laravel and MySQL, implementing features such as tour packages, reservation management, and payment processing to automate scheduling and reduce manual errors.

In addition to development, I led project management activities—coordinating with stakeholders, creating a Gantt chart to track tasks and milestones, and developing a risk management plan to identify and mitigate potential project risks. This dual role as developer and project manager ensured that the platform was delivered on schedule while maintaining alignment with business goals.

The resulting website streamlines the reservation process, reduces administrative workload, and provides a seamless experience for both customers and the company's operational team.
travelWebPM.tools: Laravel, MySQL, Google Docs, Gantt Chart, Spreadsheet, Miro

web.caption: Payment Confirmation Page with Pending Status (Admin View)
Reservation Confirmation Page with On Process Status (Admin View)
Reservation with On Process Status - Awaiting Admin Confirmation & Payment (Customer View)

professionalSyrvey.title: Data Professional Survey Dashboard
professionalSyrvey.description: Interactive dashboard analyzing survey data from data professionals on job satisfaction, salaries, and tool preferences.
professionalSyrvey.fullDescription:
This project analyzes survey data collected from data professionals to understand job satisfaction levels, salary distributions, programming language preferences, and perceptions of the industry. The goal was to gain insights into what factors influence career satisfaction and how professionals navigate the data field.

Using a publicly available dataset, I performed data cleaning and transformation using Power Query within Power BI to prepare the data for analysis. I then built an interactive dashboard using Power BI to visualize key metrics—such as participant demographics, salary distributions by job title and gender, programming language preferences, work-life balance satisfaction, and perceived difficulty of breaking into the field.

The dashboard provides a comprehensive overview of survey responses, helping viewers understand patterns in job satisfaction, compensation, and tool preferences among data professionals. This project demonstrates my ability to work with survey data, perform data wrangling, and translate raw data into meaningful visual insights.

data.caption: Data Professional Survey Dashboard - Job Satisfaction, Salary & Tool Preferences

brainysProject.title: Brainys Project
brainysProject.description: Product Requirement Document and project management for AI-powered syllabus generation platform.
brainysProject.fullDescription:
Brainys is an AI-powered syllabus generation system designed to help Indonesian teachers create syllabus components quickly and accurately. The platform addresses the need for efficient, adaptable syllabus creation that aligns with recent educational standards in Indonesia.

During my internship as an Associate Product Manager, I collaborated with cross-functional teams—including design, data, developers, and the Product Manager—to define and document the product requirements. I created the Product Requirement Document (PRD) that outlined the system's key features, including AI-powered syllabus generation, adaptability to Indonesian syllabus formats, user-friendly interface, and quick output. I also developed user personas to ensure the product was designed with the end user in mind.

I led daily standup meetings to track project progress, identify blockers, and ensure alignment across teams. During these sessions, I documented Minutes of Meeting (MoM) to capture key information, including what each team member had completed, what they planned to work on next, and any blockers they were facing. These records helped maintain transparency and provided clear follow-up for subsequent meetings.

Progress was tracked using a kanban board, where tasks were visualized from backlog to completion. I also contributed to system analysis activities, collaborating with the team to design use case diagrams, activity diagrams, and document functional requirements—ensuring that business needs were translated into clear technical specifications.

Through structured documentation and consistent project tracking, I helped ensure that Brainys Oasys development remained on schedule, delivering a solution that meets the needs of Indonesian teachers while maintaining accountability throughout the project lifecycle.
brainysProject.tools: Kanban Board, Miro, Visual Paradigm, Google Docs, Spreadsheet, Canva

techWriting.caption: Minutes of Meeting - Daily Standup (Progress, Plans, Blockers)

dedikasiLMS.title: DEDIKASI - Career Development Platform
dedikasiLMS.description: Learning management system empowering fresh graduates with information systems skills through online courses.
dedikasiLMS.fullDescription:
Indonesia faces a challenge where the working-age population (69.3% of total population) requires quality human resources to compete in a dynamic job market. However, unemployment among diploma and university graduates remains at 5.10%, indicating a gap between academic knowledge and workplace readiness. Additionally, Indonesia ranks 45th out of 64 countries in digital competitiveness, highlighting the need for digital upskilling.

DEDIKASI was developed as a free learning platform to address these challenges, empowering fresh graduates—particularly in the information systems field—with relevant digital skills. The platform allows users to explore specialized categories within information systems, helping them enhance their capabilities and improve their employability.

As the Project Manager, I led the development team using Agile methodologies. I managed the Jira board, tracking tasks and ensuring sprint progress remained on schedule. I helped coordinate daily scrums, sprint planning, sprint reviews, and retrospectives to maintain team alignment and continuous improvement. Communication was coordinated through Slack, with documentation managed in Google Docs. I also contributed to development by coding several features, collaborating with the team to ensure consistent implementation across the platform.

The resulting platform provides fresh graduates with accessible, structured learning opportunities in information systems, helping bridge the gap between academic knowledge and industry demands.
dedikasiLMS.tools: Laravel, MySQL, Jira, Slack, Google Docs

schoolSystem.title: OASYS School Project
schoolSystem.description: Comprehensive school management system for teachers, principals, admin staff, and parents.
schoolSystem.fullDescription:
Oasys School is an integrated school management system designed to streamline academic and administrative tasks for educators, principals, staff, and parents. The platform addresses the need for efficiency in managing teaching materials, student data, attendance, digital report cards, and administrative approvals.

During my internship as an Associate Product Manager, I led product definition and documentation for Oasys School. I created the Product Requirement Document (PRD) outlining key features—QR code-based attendance, digital report cards, teaching material management, and multi-platform accessibility—and developed user personas for four user roles: subject teachers, homeroom teachers, principals, and administrative staff. I also mapped the business model to align user needs with product features. The PRD and functional requirements spanned nearly 100 pages, reflecting the complexity of the system and the comprehensive planning required.

I led daily standup meetings to track progress and document Minutes of Meeting (MoM), capturing completed tasks, next steps, and blockers. For system analysis, I designed multiple activity diagrams covering various workflows—including creating teaching materials, managing attendance, and digital report card grades, among others—to translate business needs into clear technical specifications. I also developed a comprehensive manual book and guideline for end users across all roles. Through structured documentation and consistent project tracking, I ensured Oasys School development remained on schedule, delivering a solution that enhances operational efficiency for schools.
schoolSystem.tools: Kanban Board, Miro, Visual Paradigm, Google Docs, Canva

sa.caption: Activity Diagram - Managing Digital Report Card Grades

pmo.caption: Minutes of Meeting - Daily Standup (Progress, Plans, Blockers)

blogWebsite.title: Golf Leaderboard & Schedule Website
blogWebsite.description: Flask-based web application displaying golf tournament schedules and leaderboards using external API integration.
blogWebsite.fullDescription:
This project is a Flask web application that provides golf tournament schedules and leaderboard data fetched from an external API. The platform addresses the need for easily accessible and well-organized golf event information, presenting fixtures, player rankings, and scores in a clean, user-friendly interface.

I developed the back-end using Python Flask, integrating with the Golf Leaderboard Data API via the Requests library to fetch tournament schedules, player standings, and match results. The application features tournament schedules with details such as event name, country, course, type, and dates, as well as leaderboards displaying player rankings with scores and rounds. I implemented search functionality to filter fixtures by keyword and sorting options for leaderboard data to enhance usability.

The front-end was built using Jinja2 templating with Bootstrap and custom CSS to ensure a responsive and visually appealing layout. This project demonstrates my ability to work with external APIs, handle dynamic data rendering, and build a functional web application with a clean user interface.
blogWebsite.tools: Python, Flask, Jinja2, Jupyter Notebook, Requests API

web.caption: Search Feature on Schedule Page - Keyword Filtering

totalPayPrediction.title: Transjakarta Total Pay Prediction with ARIMA
totalPayPrediction.description: Built an end-to-end data analytics and forecasting pipeline using Transjakarta transaction data from Kaggle, applying data cleaning, synthetic time-series expansion, and ARIMA modeling.
totalPayPrediction.fullDescription:
This project was developed to demonstrate an end-to-end data analytics and forecasting pipeline using Transjakarta transaction data sourced from Kaggle. The original dataset contained only a very limited number of observations, making it insufficient for time series modeling such as ARIMA. In real-world scenarios, analysts often face incomplete or limited data, so this project addresses that challenge by applying data cleaning, aggregation, and synthetic time-series expansion to enable meaningful forecasting. The objective was not only to generate predictions but also to showcase a structured analytical approach that reflects real data analysis workflows.

I started by cleaning the raw transaction-level dataset, including handling missing values, removing duplicate records based on transaction ID, and ensuring consistent data types. The data was then aggregated into daily revenue to form a proper time series. Due to the limited number of original observations, I implemented a synthetic data generation approach using controlled noise to extend the dataset while preserving realistic patterns. After preparing the data, I conducted exploratory analysis, performed stationarity testing, and built an ARIMA model to capture temporal dependencies. The entire workflow was structured in a reproducible notebook, reflecting professional practices such as iterative analysis, clear documentation, and organized data pipelines.

The outputs of this project include a cleaned transaction dataset, an expanded time-series dataset with a synthetic data indicator, and forecasting results saved into CSV files, including both test predictions and a 14-day future forecast. Supporting visualizations such as time series plots and forecast comparisons were also generated to aid interpretation. The ARIMA model produced relatively stable forecasts around the recent equilibrium level, indicating that no strong temporal patterns were detected in the data.

data.caption: Forecasting Model and Save to CSV
